confirmed.note: Botched 'resolve conflicts' commit re-added +1590 lines to imports.cpp that the earlier clean split 2c74f0a (excluded) had MOVED out into kernel/threading.cpp, synchronization.cpp, nt.cpp, video.cpp... — re-pasting moved functions verbatim. E.g. KeDelayExecutionThread body is byte-identical in imports.cpp and threading.cpp. 0aecaea is a descendant of 2c74f0a yet undoes the split, leaving genuine cross-file duplication (and an ODR/redefinition hazard).

false_positives.what_happened: The install/hashes/*.cpp files are generated data-tables (line 1: '// File automatically generated by fshasher'). Each is identical scaffolding: a fixed header (#include <utility>/<array>/<cstdint>), three extern decls renamed per level (HoloskaHashes/HoloskaFiles/HoloskaFilesSize -> Mazuri.../Spagonia...), and a long std::array of '{{0}}' / hash literals. The renamed-identifier + boilerplate-header + repeated-row structure mimics edited-copy: high token w with renamed ids, plus near-identical line shape across files.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: honor a generated-file header marker — skip files whose first N lines match /generated by|@generated|DO NOT EDIT/i (the fshasher banner here), same skip already specced in backlog/completed/065_min_dedup_skip_generated.md. Secondary guard: classify a block as data-table when >70% of lines are pure aggregate-initializer rows (matching /^\s*\{.*\},?$/ or repeated '{{0}}') and demote/suppress it.

false_positives.what_happened: archcheck SF.9 reports a cycle registry.h -> registry_android.inl -> registry.h. This is the standard 'header includes its platform .inl at the bottom; the .inl re-includes the owning header so it parses standalone in an IDE' idiom — not a real circular module dependency. The .inl is a template-impl fragment, never a translation unit, so there is no real edge for layering purposes.
false_positives.fix_proposal: graph: do not treat *.inl / *.ipp / *.tcc as cycle-participating nodes — fold them into their single includer (an .inl with exactly one includer is an impl-fragment, collapse the edge). Equivalently, exclude back-edges where one endpoint is an .inl included by the very header it re-includes. Prevents the .inl<->header self-loop from inflating sccs_cyclic.

intro_commits.edge: game_window.h adds #include <sdl_events.h> while sdl_events.h (renamed from ui/window_events.h) keeps #include <ui/game_window.h>
intro_commits.reason: SDL/HID cleanup (#224) renamed ui/window_events.h -> sdl_events.h and made the event header pull in game_window.h while game_window.h still includes the event header: a careless bidirectional header dependency between window and event-pump. Real author code (UnleashedRecomp app), not vendor. NOTE: 7b9b424 is in the round1 EXCLUDE list; reported here only as the cycle-introducing edge for completeness, not counted as a new confirmed copypaste.

Android platform support (google-labs-jules[bot]) introduced the header-includes-its-.inl / .inl-re-includes-header idiom. Author code but a benign FP cycle (see false_positives). Counted as an FP, not a real structural cycle.

cycle.evidence: Both SCCs are in author code under UnleashedRecomp/ (not vendor thirdparty/imgui/MacKernelSDK/plume). sdl_events.h<->ui/game_window.h is a genuine author cycle; registry.h<->registry_android.inl is the .inl-back-include idiom (FP).
